sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: EHA: J&J sharpens myeloma edge as Talvey, Darzalex Faspro combo proves its worth in earlier disease stage
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson presented positive Phase 3 results for its Talvey and Darzalex Faspro combination therapy in earlier-stage multiple myeloma at the European Hematology Association (EHA) annual meeting. The regimen significantly reduced the risk of disease progression or death by up to 72% and death by up to 53% compared to standard therapy. This win marks the first time a GPRC5D bispecific antibody combo has shown superior progression-free survival in earlier-line multiple myeloma, leading J&J to pursue regulatory approval for earlier use.

feed.title: Lymphoma drug combo cut risk of progression or death 25%
feed.summary: Incyte announced positive results from its pivotal Phase 3 frontMIND trial, showing that the tafasitamab (Monjuvi/Minjuvi) combination therapy significantly prolonged progression-free survival in patients with previously untreated high-risk diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) or high-grade B-cell lymphoma (HGBL). The treatment reduced the risk of disease progression or death by 25% and was generally well-tolerated. These results support global regulatory applications for the therapy as a potential new standard of care.
